synthesized_reason: Phase A pass; 56/56 e2e subtests + 152 total merge-test assertions green. Legitimate medium: AC-10 fixture split. Orchestration pattern is intentional. Follow-up: unify AC-10 fixture coverage.

real_findings.note: AC-10 calls for a single e2e fixture with install-artifact contamination + protected-file conflict running through all five subphases. Current impl splits this: the contaminated fixture stops at unapproved-execute, and the happy-path approved-execute+verify uses a separate safe fixture with no contamination. Follow-up: merge the two fixtures or add a third that combines contamination + conflict + approved execute + verify.

No top-level frw merge CLI orchestrator; /furrow:merge slash command orchestrates via Claude. This is architectural by design (Claude is the human-in-the-loop approver between phases), not a spec violation. A future frw merge wrapper for CI scenarios is a follow-up.
SC3001 process substitution in merge-sort-union.sh under #!/bin/sh. Minor.

false_positives: spec-compliance: "missing orchestrator" — see orchestration-design note above. By design.
code-quality (SC2148 on script-guard.sh): hook fragment, not a standalone script.

reviewers.phase_b_fresh_claude: skipped: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY absent; codex-only satisfies generator-evaluator separation via cross-model
